description: Implement a dynamic per-tile lighting system using Canvas compositing. Light sources (room lights, player flashlight, fires) emit colored light that falls off with distance. This is the highest-ROI visual investment per the spec research.
acceptance_criteria: Each tile has a computed light color and intensity value based on nearby light sources.
Light intensity falls off with distance from the source (linear or inverse-square).
Multiple light sources blend additively on overlapping tiles.
The player carries a flashlight that emits a cone-shaped light in their facing direction.
Unpowered rooms are dark — only the player's flashlight illuminates them.
Powered rooms have ambient warm-amber light from ceiling light sources defined in the room template.
The lighting overlay is rendered via a separate offscreen canvas composited with globalCompositeOperation 'multiply' or equivalent.
spec_section: A.2.2 Color Palette Strategy, A.2.3 Layered Rendering Pipeline (Layer 5)
notes: Spec Appendix A says: 'Dynamic per-tile color lighting is the single highest-ROI visual investment for a 2D roguelike.' Use offscreen canvas + multiply composite for performance.
